headlines: Pakistani strikes kill dozens in Afghanistan -- The Taliban government says civilians were killed, while Pakistan maintains they targeted militants.
Europe's heatwave linked to 1,300 deaths, WHO says, as Germany hits record 41.7C -- WHO chief Tedros Adhanom Ghebreyesus warned that Europe is not prepared for high temperatures.
South Korea football coach quits as president calls for probe into World Cup loss -- South Korea was eliminated after missing out on a spot among the eight best third-placed teams.
Australian man charged with murder after dead girl found in suitcase in Thailand -- The body of Tunchanok Donhomla, 17, was found discarded near a railway track on Saturday, police say.
Eleven killed after plane carrying skydivers crashes in eastern France -- The pilot and 10 passengers - including five first-time parachutists - died in the incident, local officials said.

Venezuela earthquake: father and son found alive in rubble after four days as death toll nears 1,500

Venezuela's US-backed government faces quake test -- Critics say the response from Interim President Delcy Rodriguez's government to the earthquakes has been slow and inept,
Pakistani strikes kill 36 civilians in Afghanistan -- Pakistani forces' ground operations and airstrikes killed at least **36 civilians** in Afghanistan overnight and wounded
Peru's Fujimori wins presidency by slim margin -- Peru's conservative Keiko Fujimori has won the 7 June presidential runoff, defeating left-wing candidate Roberto Sanchez
Japanese yen hits 40-year low against dollar -- The Japanese yen weakened to its lowest level against the US dollar since **1986** on Tuesday, falling to **162.19 per d

An explosion from an explosive device seriously injured three people, including a Ukrainian construction tycoon and his family, at a residential building in Monaco on Monday night; the suspect fled on foot into neighbouring France and was identified via video surveillance.
WhatsApp is rolling out usernames globally to its three billion account holders over the next few months, letting users connect without sharing phone numbers, with names limited to 35 characters.

A new report from Ramp and Revelio Labs found that companies spending heavily on AI - defined as an average of $30 per employee per month - saw headcount increase 10.2%, including a 12% rise in entry-level roles, though the authors caution the findings do not show AI universally creates jobs.

Offenders in England and Wales who kill a current or ex-partner will face a new minimum sentence of 25 years - an increase of 10 years - under plans announced by justice secretary David Lammy following a seven-year campaign by victims' mothers.
[CFO] Bank Rate held at 3.75% in June 2026
Roof collapse kills 14 children in Pakistan -- **Fourteen children** died after the roof of a building near Lahore collapsed on top of them at a private tuition centre

Chinese tycoon Guo Wengui jailed 30 years for fraud -- Self-exiled Chinese billionaire **Guo Wengui** was sentenced to **30 years** in a US prison for a massive financial frau
Mothers' campaign wins domestic murder sentencing reform -- The campaign was led by Carole Gould, whose 17-year-old daughter Ellie was killed by fellow sixth-former Thomas Griffith

Gojek co-founder and former Indonesian Education Minister Nadiem Makarim has been sentenced to 10 years in prison after being convicted of corruption; Makarim says the verdict is politically motivated and plans to appeal.
